claim: AI governance means making high-level principles actionable through clear processes and workflows - it's not bureaucracy but clarity of expectations
actionable: When implementing AI governance, focus on translating principles into specific workflows and expectations for engineers at decision points
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Panel: Building Trustworthy AI through Governance, Literacy, and Community

claim: Less than 11% of companies have strong AI governance despite 80-90% using AI - governance is the biggest gap
actionable: Implement AI governance as a competitive advantage; your organization likely has a governance gap your competitors haven't filled
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Panel: Building Trustworthy AI through Governance, Literacy, and Community

claim: CEO and C-suite buy-in on AI governance is the highest indicator of success for generative AI tools according to McKinsey 2025 study
actionable: Prioritize getting executive alignment on AI governance as the foundation for successful AI deployment
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Panel: Building Trustworthy AI through Governance, Literacy, and Community

claim: Nine categories of real AI risks exist: privacy, workforce displacement, hallucinations, and others - acknowledge them and discuss mitigation strategies
actionable: When building trustworthy AI, identify which risk categories apply to your product and communicate mitigation strategies
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Panel: Building Trustworthy AI through Governance, Literacy, and Community

claim: Red teaming exercises should not be turned into media sensations - journalists exaggerate AI dangers beyond what was actually observed
actionable: When conducting red teaming, be careful about how findings are communicated externally to avoid sensationalism that misleads public perception
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Panel: Building Trustworthy AI through Governance, Literacy, and Community

claim: Creating sandbox environments with guardrails (no external shipping, no sensitive data, limited budgets) allows teams to move fast safely
actionable: Set up protected sandbox environments where teams can experiment with AI systems before requiring full governance approval
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Panel: Building Trustworthy AI through Governance, Literacy, and Community

claim: Too many governance approvals (legal, marketing, privacy, GDPR) create gridlock; sandboxes solve this by preemptively defining safe boundaries
actionable: Instead of requiring multiple approvals per project, define safe experimentation zones where approvals are pre-granted
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Panel: Building Trustworthy AI through Governance, Literacy, and Community

claim: Immigration restrictions harm US AI development - many AI engineers are immigrants who came on student visas or H1Bs
actionable: Advocate for immigration policies that attract AI talent; this is one of the highest-leverage policy levers for AI development
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Panel: Building Trustworthy AI through Governance, Literacy, and Community
